# Study Design Framework

The study design converts a confirmed research spine and exemplar adaptation plan into an evidence- or warrant-producing action plan.

Read `design-family-grammars.md` before selecting field-specific requirements.

## Common design components

| Component | Required Question |
|---|---|
| Research question | What exactly will be answered? |
| Theory or conceptual orientation | What role do concepts, theory, assumptions, or values play? |
| Unit, case, population, or corpus | What is studied, interpreted, compared, traced, or argued about? |
| Selection logic | Why these participants, cases, sites, periods, documents, datasets, sources, or experiments? |
| Evidence source or material | What supports the answer? |
| Evidence generation or source construction | How is the material produced, gathered, screened, or assembled? |
| Analysis or interpretation | How does the material become a finding, explanation, interpretation, synthesis, or argument? |
| Comparison or warrant | What baseline, counterfactual, contrast, negative case, rival explanation, source criticism, counterargument, or premise supports the inference? |
| Evaluation or credibility criterion | What counts as adequate support in this design family? |
| Sensitivity or scope check | Would the claim survive plausible alternatives or contextual changes? |
| Ethics, access, and researcher position | Which responsibilities and constraints shape the inquiry? |
| Inference boundary | Which conclusion and generalization mode are permitted? |

Use the rows that serve the selected research design.

## Adaptive terminology

| Specialized term | Field-general function |
|---|---|
| Baseline/control | Comparison, counterfactual, contrast, negative case, rival explanation, or counterargument |
| Evaluation metric | Evaluation, credibility, or warrant criterion |
| Robustness check | Sensitivity, triangulation, dependability, rival-explanation, or scope check |
| Failure case | Null result, disconfirming evidence, negative case, counterexample, or boundary condition |
| Figure plan | Evidence-display plan |
| Generalization | Statistical generalization, analytical generalization, theoretical transfer, or contextual transferability |

## User action requirement

Each study component should state:

```text
what the user must do
  -> which reference-paper function it adapts
  -> what material or evidence it produces
  -> which claim or scope boundary it serves
  -> how completion is evaluated
```

The plan should be executable by a user who recognizes the target paper but has not yet internalized its design logic.

## Validity matching

- Quantitative descriptive and associational designs emphasize construct validity, sampling, measurement, model specification, uncertainty, confounding, and bounded association.
- Quantitative causal designs add estimands, identification assumptions, assignment or exposure logic, counterfactuals, falsification, and causal scope.
- Qualitative designs emphasize context, purposive selection, researcher role, data-generation transparency, analysis development, reflexivity, negative cases, credibility, and transferability.
- Case, historical, archival, and process designs emphasize case/source selection, chronology, provenance, source criticism, within-case evidence, rival explanations, and analytical generalization.
- Mixed methods emphasizes the role of each strand, sequence, integration point, joint display, divergence, and meta-inference.
- Conceptual, critical, and normative work emphasizes definitions, premises, argument sequence, counterarguments, examples or counterexamples, value criteria, and applicability.
- Review and synthesis designs emphasize corpus construction, screening, appraisal, synthesis, debate mapping, and corpus limits.
- Computational and experimental work retains dataset/material provenance, controls, metrics, uncertainty, replication, ablations, robustness, and leakage or implementation checks.

## Output rule

Each design step should support a claim, rule out an alternative, establish credibility, produce an evidence display, or define scope. Conventional steps without a research function are omitted.
